meaning: adv.急速地adj.快速的n.急速的乐章
examples: It is allegro from Concerto Number One "Spring" from the "Four Seasons".
Set against the compulsory mobility and clogged roads of "the great car economy", the Allegro age now has a certain perverse appeal.
The first broad preludio gives way to a bouncing allegro.
Switzerland broke the Scot's serve once more before Allegro eventually served out for victory.
At Chicago's recently remodeled Allegro Hotel, living the good life is part of the deal.
